name: The Misty Mountains
description: The high passes and flanks of the Hithaeglir. Altitude weather — thin air, sudden storms, snow possible in any season above the treeline. Old and unfriendly country; the mountains themselves seem to resent travellers.

spring.label: Clear and cold, the peaks sharp-edged against a pale sky.
Mist clinging to the peaks, the path visible only a bow-shot ahead.
Thaw running down the slopes — every path a running stream of meltwater.
A heavy late snowfall, the passes closed for a day or more.
A cruel wind from the heights, impossible to stand against on exposed ground.
A storm arriving with no warning — wind, driven snow, nowhere to shelter.
A brief fair spell, the Anduin Vales visible far below to the east.
Sleet and freezing rain, the stones glazed and treacherous.

summer.label: Fair and bright, the air thin and cold even under the sun.
Heavy morning mist in the valleys, burning away as the sun climbs.
A sudden mountain storm — black cloud boiling over the peaks, lightning close.
A keen wind along the ridges, cloud-shadow racing across the slopes.
Cold steady rain — reminder that this is no summer in the lowlands' sense.
Snow falling on the higher peaks, rain at travel-altitude.
A rare still clear day — the mountains at their most beautiful and least honest.
A wind that whistles strangely through the high places — the old tales stir.

autumn.label: Cold and clear, first snow already whitening the highest peaks.
The first true mountain storm of the season — snow, wind, a warning.
Freezing mist, hoarfrost growing on cloak and beard.
A cutting wind from the north, the cold working through every layer.
Steady snowfall at altitude — the passes beginning to close.
Freezing rain, every rock a sheet of ice underfoot.
Low grey cloud sitting on the slopes, damp and cold and close.
A brief fair spell between storms — seize it or regret it.

winter.label: Blizzard — the passes impassable, shelter the only hope, visibility nothing.
Deep fresh snow, every step laboured, every hour of progress hard-won.
A killing cold — still, clear, absolute. Skin freezes to metal in moments.
A shrieking wind along the ridges, drifting snow across every path.
Frozen fog, the world grey and close, every surface rimed white.
Steady heavy snow — not a storm, but relentless, hour after hour.
Clear and brilliantly cold, the sun no comfort at all.
A storm with no natural feel to it — fell voices in the wind, the old malice.
